## Task 2: RagConfig in corvia-common

Add the `[rag]` section to `CorviaConfig` so it can be read from `corvia.toml`.

**Files:**
- Modify: `crates/corvia-common/src/config.rs`

**Step 1: Write the failing test**

Add to the `#[cfg(test)]` module at the bottom of `crates/corvia-common/src/config.rs`:

```rust
#[test]
fn test_rag_config_defaults() {
    let config = CorviaConfig::default();
    assert_eq!(config.rag.default_limit, 10);
    assert!(config.rag.graph_expand);
    assert_eq!(config.rag.graph_depth, 2);
    assert!((config.rag.graph_alpha - 0.3).abs() < f32::EPSILON);
    assert!((config.rag.reserve_for_answer - 0.2).abs() < f32::EPSILON);
    assert_eq!(config.rag.max_context_tokens, 0);
    assert!(config.rag.system_prompt.is_none());
}

#[test]
fn test_rag_config_from_toml() {
    let toml_str = r#"
[project]
name = "test"
org = "test-org"
scope_id = "test"

[storage]
store_type = "lite"
data_dir = ".corvia"

[embedding]
provider = "ollama"
url = "http://127.0.0.1:11434"
model = "nomic-embed-text"
dimensions = 768

[server]
host = "127.0.0.1"
port = 8020

[rag]
default_limit = 20
graph_expand = false
graph_alpha = 0.5
max_context_tokens = 8192
system_prompt = "You are a Corvia assistant."
"#;
    let config: CorviaConfig = toml::from_str(toml_str).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(config.rag.default_limit, 20);
    assert!(!config.rag.graph_expand);
    assert!((config.rag.graph_alpha - 0.5).abs() < f32::EPSILON);
    assert_eq!(config.rag.max_context_tokens, 8192);
    assert_eq!(config.rag.system_prompt.as_deref(), Some("You are a Corvia assistant."));
}
```

**Step 2: Run tests to verify they fail**

Run: `cargo test -p corvia-common test_rag_config -- --nocapture`
Expected: FAIL — `no field rag on type CorviaConfig`
